title: Electrical and Electronic Equipment Assemblers
description: Assemble or modify electrical or electronic equipment, such as computers, test equipment telemetering systems, electric motors, and batteries.

tasks.text: Read and interpret schematic drawings, diagrams, blueprints, specifications, work orders, or reports to determine materials requirements or assembly instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble electrical or electronic systems or support structures and install components, units, subassemblies, wiring, or assembly casings, using rivets, bolts, soldering or micro-welding equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust, repair, or replace electrical or electronic components to correct defects and to ensure conformance to specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position, align, or adjust workpieces or electrical parts to facilitate wiring or assembly.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Explain assembly procedures or techniques to other workers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean parts, using cleaning solutions, air hoses, and cloths.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drill or tap holes in specified equipment locations to mount control units or to provide openings for elements, wiring, or instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fabricate or form parts, coils, or structures according to specifications, using drills, calipers, cutters, or saws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with supervisors or engineers to plan or review work activities or to resolve production problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect or test wiring installations, assemblies, or circuits for resistance factors or for operation, and record results.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Mark and tag components so that stock inventory can be tracked and identified.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure and adjust voltages to specified values to determine operational accuracy of instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Complete, review, or maintain production, time, or component waste reports.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Distribute materials, supplies, or subassemblies to work areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pack finished assemblies for shipment, and transport them to storage areas, using hoists or handtrucks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Instruct customers in the installation, repair, or maintenance of products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
